content:
Question: did anyone get LSP and/or Flycheck in Emacs to work for Rust in the kernel?

For me it works out-of-the-box when I open sources in a Cargo project. But as the kernel does not use Cargo it gets confused. I couldn't figure it out, yet, how to make it work.

Did someone have success with that? And if yes, how? :)

I have rust-analyzer running via eglot. wanted to also look at flycheck, but did not yet manage to.

did you run the rust-analyzer make target? (I do it with O=build and then ln -s build/rust-analyzer.json .)

Fiona Behrens schrieb:

did you run the rust-analyzer make target? (I do it with O=build and then ln -s build/rust-analyzer.json .)

I did just now :) But I don't find a build/rust-analyzer.json.
are you building with or without a O= flag to make?

Fiona Behrens schrieb:

are you building with or without a O= flag to make?

With. But there is no file with this name in the source nor in the build tree. Even a grep for the name on whole tree came up empty.
for me the rust-analyzer make target creates a rust-project.json in the root

Benno Lossin schrieb:

for me the rust-analyzer make target creates a rust-project.json in the root

Yes, this one exists.
Yeah that's what I meant with it does honor O=build, if you build without O= you can just use that file directly
Yes, Eglot works now. LSP seems to get into an endless loop scanning the build tree. Not sure what's wrong, but certainly a big step forward...
I’m running emacs with rustic-mode and lsp-mode. I had to fork rustic locally a few days ago because they made some changes so that the thing won’t load for non cargo projects. Something in flycheck.
Some kind of clippy flycheck mode that stops everything from loading if it does not find a cargo manifest.

Andreas Hindborg schrieb:

I’m running emacs with rustic-mode and lsp-mode

So lsp-mode alone works for you? As mentioned, for me it hangs up in a loop scanning the tree.

U

Oliver Mangold said:

Andreas Hindborg schrieb:

I’m running emacs with rustic-mode and lsp-mode

So lsp-mode alone works for you? As mentioned, for me it hangs up in a loop scanning the tree.

One I produce the json file and move it from build tree to source tree, it works fine. I disable directory watching though, too many directories.

Andreas Hindborg schrieb:

I disable directory watching though, too many directories.

That explains it. After that it works for me, too. Thanks a lot.
An additional question, should Rust-Analyzer also work on bindings_generated.rs with that setup? For me it complains that it 'is not included anywhere in the module tree'.
@Tamir Duberstein recently sent a patch to list to enable that.
https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250210-rust-analyzer-bindings-include-v2-2-23dff845edc3@gmail.com/#r
I have it in the rnull-v6.14-rc1 tree, so if you have been using that it might have worked. But if you fork upstream, you won't get completions for bindings:: if you build out of tree.
Yes, that fixes it. I will just work on this version your tree, then.

You can work on upstream, just apply the patch. I can recommend b4 for the task:

cd my-linux-checkout
b4 shazam 20250210-rust-analyzer-bindings-include-v2-2-23dff845edc3@gmail.com

I have a patched version of b4 that allows you to set a prefix for the subject line like so:

b4 shazam -p LIST 20250210-rust-analyzer-bindings-include-v2-2-23dff845edc3@gmail.com

Then it will be applied to your tree with a prefix. Like here I applied some patches enabling werror for clippy below my hrtimer tree:

5ea6d64724e08 * rust: sync: add `Arc::as_ptr`
c2aac5b2f922e * rust: hrtimer: introduce hrtimer support
b851c1bef1a6d * rust: time: Add Ktime::from_ns()
1079a5cc062b5 * hrtimer Rust API
988bd1ba2ebb3 * LIST: [PATCH 3/3] kbuild: rust: support `W=e` for Rust
3b39f5625406b * LIST: [PATCH 2/3] kbuild: rust: apply `CONFIG_WERROR` to all Rust targets
89cfa7f9c1099 * LIST: [PATCH 1/3] kbuild: rust: move `-Dwarnings` handling to `Makefile.extrawarn`
2014c95afecee * v6.14-rc1 torvalds/master Linux 6.14-rc1
That makes it easy to see what patches are something you picked up from list. You can also add tags like "LOCAL" for things that should not be submitted.
b4 branch here: https://github.com/metaspace/b4/tree/prefix
